title: Racinaea almeriae
intro: Racinaea almeriae, also known as the Almirante's puya, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and robust leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Racinaea almeriae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Racinaea almeriae is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin care.
taxonomy: Racinaea almeriae is a species within the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically the monocots. The genus Racinaea belongs to the subfamily Tillandsioideae, which includes various epiphytic and terrestrial species. Within the subfamily, Racinaea almeriae is one of the many species found, often characterized by their unique growth habits and flower structures. Further classification within the plant kingdom positions Racinaea almeriae as a member of the kingdom Plantae.

parts: Racinaea almeriae, also known as Almeria Air Plant, is a species of air plant native to Mexico. It is primarily used as an ornamental plant due to its vibrant colors and unique growth habits.
